tool.name: Masking table
tool.description: pivot per-masker bedtools genomecov into a MultiQC custom-content table (% genome masked)
tool.command: python '$__tool_directory__/masking_table.py' --dustmasker #echo " ".join(["%s=%s" % (str($el.element_identifier), str($el)) for $el in $dustmasker])# --windowmasker #echo " ".join(["%s=%s" % (str($el.element_identifier), str($el)) for $el in $windowmasker])# --tantan #echo " ".join(["%s=%s" % (str($el.element_identifier), str($el)) for $el in $tantan])# --fastan #echo " ".join(["%s=%s" % (str($el.element_identifier), str($el)) for $el in $fastan])# #if $union --union #echo " ".join(["%s=%s" % (str($el.element_identifier), str($el)) for $el in $union])# #end if --out '$output'

param.name: dustmasker
param.label: dustmasker genomecov (per strain)
param.help: bedtools genomecov (hist, -max 1) output for dustmasker intervals; element identifier = strain.

param.name: union
param.label: union genomecov (per strain, optional)
param.help: bedtools genomecov for the merged union of all maskers. Adds a 'union' column.

tool.help: Reads each masker's per-strain ``bedtools genomecov`` (hist, ``-max 1``) output, takes the genome-wide masked fraction (the ``genome 1 ...`` row, column 5), converts to a percent, and writes a tab-separated matrix with one row per strain and one column per masker (plus ``union`` if provided). The header line is plain (no leading ``#``) so MultiQC custom-content (plot type = table) renders it correctly.
